title: AGENTS.md — commaKit AI Context
text: commaKit (Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit) R/Bioconductor-style package analyzing bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports genome-wide characterization methylation patterns across multiple modification types (6mA, 5mC, 4mC), annotation methylation sites relative genomic features, identification differentially methylated sites conditions. R package namespace: commaKit GitHub repo: carl-stone/commaKit Version: 0.2.0 R: >= 4.3.0 (CI pinned R 4.5) License: MIT

title: AGENTS.md — commaKit AI Context
text: commaData extends RangedSummarizedExperiment. Genomic positions stored GRanges rowRanges() (1-bp ranges per site). Methylation fractions coverage counts assay matrices (rows = sites, columns = samples). Key accessors: - methylation(object) — methylation fractions matrix (0–1) - coverage(object) — coverage count matrix (non-negative integers) - modTypes(object) — unique modification types (e.g., “6mA”, “5mC”) - modContexts(object) — unique modification+context units (e.g., “6mA:GATC”, “5mC:CCWGG”) - motifs(object) — sequence motifs per site - siteInfo(object) — flat DataFrame chrom, position, strand, mod_type, motif, computed site_key - sampleInfo(object) — sample metadata DataFrame - caller(object) — caller produced data (“modkit”, “dorado”, “megalodon”) Important: mod_context modification + context, just “modification context.” mod_type chemical modification (6mA, 5mC, 4mC); motif/context sequence context (GATC, CCWGG, CpG); mod_context two-part biological unit (e.g., 6mA:GATC). package modification-type agnostic — never assume single mod_type. Alignment: alignment genomic position (GRanges) via findOverlaps(), row names string keys. site_key computed display column (e.g., chr1:512:+:6mA:GATC), never used alignment.

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: commaKit (Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports three modification types — N6-methyladenine (6mA), 5-methylcytosine (5mC), N4-methylcytosine (4mC) — single, unified data container. vignette walks complete analysis workflow using built-comma_example_data synthetic dataset. typical commaKit workflow six steps: Load per-sample methylation files commaData object. QC data (coverage, beta distributions, PCA). Annotate sites relative genomic features. Test differential methylation conditions. Visualize results. Enrich differentially methylated genes (GO/KEGG).

title: Getting Started with commaKit
code: devtools::install_github("carl-stone/commaKit") # BiocManager::install("commaKit") after Bioconductor release library(commaKit)

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: commaData extends Bioconductor’s RangedSummarizedExperiment central data container commaKit. stores: methylation — sites × samples matrix beta values (0–1). siteCoverage — sites × samples matrix read depths. rowRanges — one 1-bp GRanges range per methylation site, mod_type motif metadata. mod_context — computed demand mod_type motif modContexts() siteInfo(). colData — per-sample metadata: sample_name, condition, replicate. Seqinfo — chromosome names, lengths, circularity metadata. annotation — genomic features GRanges object metadata(). motifSites — motif instances GRanges object metadata(). built-comma_example_data contains 588 synthetic methylation sites (393 × 6mA, 195 × 5mC) simulated 100 kb chromosome across six samples: three controls (ctrl_1, ctrl_2, ctrl_3) three treatments (treat_1, treat_2, treat_3).
code: data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 588 | samples: 6 #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA #> motifs: CCWGG, GATC #> mod contexts: 5mC_CCWGG, 6mA_GATC #> conditions: control, treatment #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total) #> annotation: 5 features #> motif sites: none #> caller: modkit #> min_coverage: 5 # Modification types present modTypes(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC" "6mA"  # Modification contexts (combines mod_type and motif) modContexts(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC_CCWGG" "6mA_GATC"  # Per-sample metadata sampleInfo(comma_example_data) #>         sample_name condition replicate caller #> ctrl_1       ctrl_1   control         1 modkit #> ctrl_2       ctrl_2   control         2 modkit #> ctrl_3       ctrl_3   control         3 modkit #> treat_1     treat_1 treatment         1 modkit #> treat_2     treat_2 treatment         2 modkit #> treat_3     treat_3 treatment         3 modkit  # Matrix dimensions: sites × samples dim(methylation(comma_example_data)) #> [1] 588   6

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_coverage() shows distribution sequencing depth per site, per sample. Consistent coverage across samples important quality indicator. Coverage depth distribution per sample.
code: plot_coverage(comma_example_data)

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_methylation_distribution() plots density methylation levels sample. Bacterial genomes often show bimodal distribution (sites either fully methylated unmethylated). Methylation beta value density per sample, faceted modification type. Restrict single modification type: Beta value density 6mA sites .
code: plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data) plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_pca() performs PCA per-sample methylation profiles. Samples condition cluster together. Internally, beta values converted M-values via mValues() PCA, stabilizes variance across sites near 0 1. PCA methylation profiles colored condition. retrieve underlying scores custom plotting, use return_data = TRUE. result data.frame PC1, PC2, sample metadata columns; percentage variance explained PC stored attr(result, "percentVar").
code: plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition") pca_df <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, return_data = TRUE) attr(pca_df, "percentVar")  # variance explained by PC1 and PC2 #>  PC1  PC2  #> 36.3 17.4

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: annotateSites() maps methylation sites genomic features, always computing four parallel list columns rowData: feature_types — GFF3 feature type association (e.g., "gene", "promoter"). feature_names — feature name association. rel_position — signed distance feature (0 = inside; negative = upstream; positive = downstream, strand-aware). frac_position — normalized position within feature ([0, 1]; NA sites outside). Use keep argument filter associations retained: "" (default), "overlap" (overlapping features), "proximity" (retains rel_position, drops frac_position), "metagene" (overlapping features frac_position). plot_metagene() visualizes average methylation profile across gene bodies: Mean methylation profile across gene bodies (TSS TTS).
code: annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data) si <- siteInfo(annotated)  # Proportion of sites overlapping at least one annotated feature mean(lengths(si$feature_names) > 0) #> [1] 0.03401361 plot_metagene(comma_example_data, feature = "gene")

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_tss_profile() shows methylation centered transcription start sites, optional regulatory element coloring: TSS-centered methylation profile.
code: plot_tss_profile(comma_example_data, feature_type = "gene")

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_genome_track() produces genome browser–style plot methylation along chromosome region: Genome track first 50 kb chr_sim.
code: plot_genome_track(comma_example_data, chromosome = "chr_sim",                   start = 1L, end = 50000L, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. v1, pass one-sided formula exactly one two-level comparison variable, ~ condition. Multi-factor formulas (~ condition + batch), interactions, offsets, continuous covariates interpreted yet; run one two-group comparison per diffMethyl() call. result object statistical results rowData active result layer.
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") cd_dm #> class: commaData #> sites: 588 | samples: 6 #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA #> motifs: CCWGG, GATC #> mod contexts: 5mC_CCWGG, 6mA_GATC #> conditions: control, treatment #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total) #> annotation: 5 features #> motif sites: none #> caller: modkit #> min_coverage: 5

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: diffMethyl() keeps method = "methylkit" default compatibility established methylKit workflows logistic-regression conventions. sensible choice already use methylKit elsewhere want results follow methylKit’s handling differential methylation. method = "quasi_f" good general-purpose alternative bacterial methylomes. uses quasibinomial model empirical Bayes dispersion shrinkage, keeps multiple-testing correction inside commaKit, can practical first alternative methylKit convergence warnings, zero-variance sites, runtime become distracting. method = "limma" uses limma’s empirical-Bayes linear model M-values. useful want familiar limma workflow complete data, especially replicates per group. three backends report dm_delta_beta original beta scale, effect sizes remain comparable even p-values differ. Extract results tidy data frame: Filter significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):
code: resultLayers(cd_dm) #> DataFrame with 1 row and 18 columns #>          name        role                   type      source is_default #>   <character> <character>            <character> <character>  <logical> #> 1  diffMethyl  diffMethyl differential_methyla..  diffMethyl       TRUE #>        method     formula   reference   treatment     mod_context #>   <character> <character> <character> <character> <CharacterList> #> 1   methylkit  ~condition     control   treatment                 #>          mod_type           motif p_adjust_method min_coverage     alpha #>   <CharacterList> <CharacterList>     <character>    <integer> <numeric> #> 1             6mA                              BH            5       0.5 #>                           result_cols              timestamp package_version #>                       <CharacterList>            <character>     <character> #> 1 dm_pvalue,dm_padj,dm_delta_beta,... 2026-06-10 13:40:43 ..           0.2.0 res <- results(cd_dm) # Top sites by adjusted p-value head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "mod_type", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>       chrom position mod_type dm_delta_beta      dm_padj #> 196 chr_sim    50176      6mA    -0.7336497 1.849154e-75 #> 287 chr_sim    70003      6mA    -0.7050844 3.896483e-68 #> 260 chr_sim    63550      6mA    -0.7799241 5.006897e-66 #> 249 chr_sim    61440      6mA    -0.7090099 1.178364e-64 #> 347 chr_sim    86016      6mA    -0.6743832 3.661541e-62 #> 9   chr_sim     2180      6mA    -0.7543758 4.024452e-60 sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Significant sites:", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites: 31

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_volcano() displays differential methylation landscape. Sites colored direction significance: Volcano plot: effect size (Δβ) vs. significance (–log10 padj).
code: plot_volcano(res)

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: plot_heatmap() shows methylation beta values top differentially methylated sites: Heatmap top 30 differentially methylated 6mA sites.
code: plot_heatmap(res, cd_dm, n_sites = 30L)

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: enrichMethylation() performs gene set enrichment differentially methylated genes. supports Gene Ontology (GO) KEGG ontologies, -representation analysis (ORA) gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) methods, distinguishes target genes (DM sites overlap gene bodies) regulator genes (whose products bind near DM sites).

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: running enrichment, sites must annotated annotateSites(): Run GO enrichment target genes — genes whose bodies overlap differentially methylated sites:
code: cd_dm <- annotateSites(cd_dm, keep = "overlap") # GO Biological Process enrichment on target genes enr <- enrichMethylation(cd_dm, ont = "BP", gene_role = "target") # Access results enr$go

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: gene_role argument controls genes classified: "target" — genes whose bodies overlap DM sites. background universe genes annotation. "regulator" — genes whose products (e.g., transcription factors) bind near DM sites. background universe regulators type. "" — runs analyses separately returns named list.

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: KEGG REST API rate limits. avoid hitting , build term--gene mapping offline cache :
code: # Build KEGG term2gene mapping (2 API calls, cache to RDS) kegg_t2g <- buildKEGGTermGene("eco", file = "kegg_eco.rds")  # Build gene ID map: symbol <-> KEGG ID (1 API call) id_map <- buildKEGGGeneIDMap("eco",     OrgDb = org.EcK12.eg.db::org.EcK12.eg.db)  # Run KEGG enrichment with offline mapping enr_kegg <- enrichMethylation(cd_dm,     kegg_term2gene = kegg_t2g$term2gene,     kegg_term2name = kegg_t2g$term2name,     gene_role = "target") enr_kegg$kegg

title: Getting Started with commaKit
text: want rank genes methylation score rather using hard threshold, use GSEA:
code: enr_gsea <- enrichMethylation(cd_dm, method = "gsea",     ont = "BP", gene_role = "target")

title: Troubleshooting Data Import
text: import problems come one four places: file format match selected caller. Sample names files colData$sample_name match exactly. Genome information missing, unnamed, uses chromosome names match methylation files. Annotation files missing optional dependencies use unexpected file extension. Start checking input sample can parsed caller sample names identical across files colData.
code: library(commaKit)  files <- c(     ctrl_1 = "ctrl_1.modkit.bed",     treat_1 = "treat_1.modkit.bed" )  col_data <- data.frame(     sample_name = c("ctrl_1", "treat_1"),     condition = c("control", "treatment"),     replicate = c(1L, 1L) )  obj <- commaData(     files = files,     colData = col_data,     genome = c(chr1 = 4641652L),     caller = "modkit" )

title: Troubleshooting Data Import
text: Use caller = "modkit" modkit pileup BED files. recommended path Dorado-based workflows: call bases Dorado, run modkit pileup, import resulting BED files commaKit. Use caller = "dorado" want commaKit parse aligned BAM file MM/ML base modification tags directly. BAM must contain modification tags indexed. Direct BAM parsing provide motif context, per-site motif values NA. Use caller = "megalodon" legacy Megalodon per-read modification BED files. Megalodon files encode modification type way commaKit can infer, provide exactly one mod_type value.
code: # Recommended path for Dorado runs after modkit pileup obj_modkit <- commaData(files, col_data, genome = c(chr1 = 4641652L),                         caller = "modkit")  # Direct Dorado BAM import obj_dorado <- commaData(bam_files, col_data, genome = c(chr1 = 4641652L),                         caller = "dorado")  # Legacy Megalodon import requires a single explicit modification type obj_megalodon <- commaData(megalodon_files, col_data,                            genome = c(chr1 = 4641652L),                            caller = "megalodon",                            mod_type = "5mC")

title: Troubleshooting Data Import
text: commaKit expects modkit pileup bedMethyl output least 18 columns header row. parser uses first 18 fields: columns 1 3: chromosome, 0-based start, end column 4: modification code, often code,motif,position column 6: strand column 10: valid coverage column 11: percent modified parser converts 0-based BED starts 1-based genomic positions converts percent modified values 0 100 beta values 0 1. Common symptoms fixes: modkit mod_code field includes motif context, ,GATC,1, commaKit stores mod_type = "6mA" motif = "GATC". Older derived files without motif context can still import, motif NA.
code: first_rows <- read.table("ctrl_1.modkit.bed", nrows = 5, fill = TRUE) ncol(first_rows) first_rows[, c(1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 10, 11)]

title: Troubleshooting Data Import
text: files must named character vector, names must match colData$sample_name. constructor also requires sample_name, condition, replicate columns colData. either result non-empty, fix names calling commaData(). Additional sample metadata columns allowed preserved sampleInfo(object).
code: setdiff(names(files), col_data$sample_name) setdiff(col_data$sample_name, names(files))

title: Troubleshooting Data Import
text: Genome information strongly recommended becomes Seqinfo attached methylation sites. Use one forms: named integer numeric vector chromosome sizes FASTA file path named Biostrings::DNAStringSet whole BSgenome object pass single unnamed sequence BSgenomeObject$NC_000913. creates DNAString without chromosome name, commaKit attach sequence length methylation sites. Use whole BSgenome object named vector instead. Chromosome names must match methylation files. BED file uses chr1 genome vector named NC_000913, rename one side import. Extra chromosomes genome input dropped message present methylation data.
code: # Named vector: simplest and robust for one bacterial chromosome genome <- c(NC_000913 = 4641652L)  # FASTA path: names come from FASTA sequence headers genome <- "E_coli_K12.fa"  # Whole BSgenome object: pass the object, not one chromosome extracted with $ genome <- BSgenome.Ecoli.NCBI.20080805::BSgenome.Ecoli.NCBI.20080805 bed_chroms <- unique(read.table("ctrl_1.modkit.bed", nrows = 1000)[[1]]) names(genome) setdiff(bed_chroms, names(genome))

title: Troubleshooting Data Import

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: Bacterial genomes frequently carry multiple simultaneous DNA methylation marks: 6mA (N6-methyladenine) — deposited Dam methyltransferase GATC motifs; functions mismatch repair cell-cycle regulation. 5mC (5-methylcytosine) — deposited Dcm methyltransferase CCWGG motifs; roles gene regulation less well characterized bacteria. 4mC (N4-methylcytosine) — deposited restriction-modification systems various sequence motifs; protects foreign DNA. commaKit stores modification types single commaData object, enabling joint analysis, comparison, visualization across modification types. vignette demonstrates multi-modification workflows using built-comma_example_data dataset, contains 6mA 5mC sites.
code: library(commaKit) data(comma_example_data)

text: default, diffMethyl() tests modification types present object. Use results(..., mod_type = "6mA") extract type-specific results:
code: dm_all <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition) res_6ma <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "6mA") res_5mc <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "5mC")  cat("6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_6ma$dm_padj) & res_6ma$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_6ma$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 31 cat("5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_5mc$dm_padj) & res_5mc$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_5mc$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 11

title: Understanding the commaData object
text: commaKit workflow functions start commaData object, many return updated commaData object added analysis layers. functions expose layers tables, plots, enrichment result lists. object stable substrate workflow: rows genomic methylation sites, columns samples, assays store measurements, later analyses add named layers biological coordinates. built-example data synthetic, exercise object model used real data. contain multiple modification types multiple sequence contexts, examples avoid assuming data set specific one methylation chemistry.

title: Understanding the commaData object
text: commaData extends Bioconductor’s RangedSummarizedExperiment. core dimensions therefore interpretation throughout package: rows one-base methylation sites; columns biological samples; assays site--sample matrices; rowRanges() stores genomic coordinates; colData() stores sample metadata; metadata() stores package-level context annotations, motif sites, caller information, analysis parameters. main package accessors expose two required assay matrices. Beta values proportions [0, 1]; coverage values read depths. Sample metadata live colData() also available sampleInfo().
code: dim(comma_example_data) #> [1] 588   6 SummarizedExperiment::assayNames(comma_example_data) #> [1] "methylation"      "coverage"         "mod_counts"       "canonical_counts" methylation(comma_example_data)[1:5, 1:3] #>         ctrl_1    ctrl_2    ctrl_3 #> [1,] 0.7575443 0.8107216 0.8870622 #> [2,] 0.9738114 0.9121264 0.8963508 #> [3,] 0.8678683 0.8581721 0.9193607 #> [4,] 0.9569443 0.9232934 0.8870824 #> [5,] 0.9165347 0.8104115 0.9441796 siteCoverage(comma_example_data)[1:5, 1:3] #>      ctrl_1 ctrl_2 ctrl_3 #> [1,]     76     93     51 #> [2,]    124     83    126 #> [3,]     88    149     55 #> [4,]     31     37     15 #> [5,]     73    130    110 sampleInfo(comma_example_data) #>         sample_name condition replicate caller #> ctrl_1       ctrl_1   control         1 modkit #> ctrl_2       ctrl_2   control         2 modkit #> ctrl_3       ctrl_3   control         3 modkit #> treat_1     treat_1 treatment         1 modkit #> treat_2     treat_2 treatment         2 modkit #> treat_3     treat_3 treatment         3 modkit

title: Understanding the commaData object
text: mod_context value read modification + context. just synonym motif. mod_type answers chemical base modification measured, 6mA, 5mC, 4mC. motif answers sequence biological context site belongs , GATC, CCWGG, CpG, future context definition. mod_context answers biological class sites analyzed, 6mA_GATC 5mC_CCWGG. built-data illustrate mod_context combination parts. caller provide motif information, motif may NA computed mod_context falls back modification type alone. Code uses modContexts() rather hard-coded labels remains compatible single-context, multi-context, future-context data sets. distinction important differential methylation. Analyses silently pool distinct mod_context groups, methylation different modification-plus-context units can reflect different biological systems.
code: modTypes(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC" "6mA" motifs(comma_example_data) #> [1] "CCWGG" "GATC" modContexts(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC_CCWGG" "6mA_GATC" context_table <- unique(as.data.frame(si[, c("mod_type", "motif", "mod_context")])) context_table[order(context_table$mod_context), ] #>     mod_type motif mod_context #> 394      5mC CCWGG   5mC_CCWGG #> 1        6mA  GATC    6mA_GATC

title: Understanding the commaData object
text: useful way learn commaKit think layers: measurement layer contains methylation coverage assays; coordinate layer rowRanges(); sample layer colData(); annotation layer adds feature relationships sites; statistical layer adds differential methylation results; plotting, filtering, enrichment functions interpret layers. object dimensions stay anchored sites samples layers added recomputed. Layering intended explicit: rerunning unnamed compatibility layer updates active/default view, explicitly named layers can coexist comparison. either case, commaKit never creates second copy biological rows. Differential methylation adds statistical result layer. default, diffMethyl() works modification contexts present object, keeps distinct modification-plus-context units separate. results() exposes active result layer tidy data frame preserving site metadata needed interpret row. keep multiple named runs, pass result result_name retrieve older layer. important pattern downstream functions replace central object model. add, expose, interpret layers attached genomic methylation-site substrate.

title: Copilot Instructions for commaKit
text: Naming: - S4 class: camelCase lowercase-first (commaData) - Analysis functions: verbNoun camelCase (annotateSites, diffMethyl) - Plot functions: plot_noun() snake_case (plot_volcano, plot_metagene) - Internal functions: . prefix (.parseBetaValues) - Arguments: snake_case (mod_type, min_coverage) - Test files: test-functionName.R Hard rules: - Every exported function takes commaData primary input - Use GenomicRanges::findOverlaps() interval overlap — never nested -loops - plot_*() return ggplot/patchwork objects (except plot_heatmap() -> ComplexHeatmap) - Genome size seqlengths(object) (Seqinfo), never hardcoded - Document every exported function roxygen2: @param, @return, @examples - Import dplyr tidyr individually — never import tidyverse (Bioconductor requirement) - use purrr::map_dfr() (superseded) — use map(...) |> list_rbind() - use purrr::map_dbl() (superseded) — use vapply() Known gotchas: - \donttest{} examples run CI — use \dontrun{} examples needing external files - diag(x) scalar x creates x*x identity matrix — use diag(x, nrow=1) - S4Vectors::rename() masks dplyr::rename() — always use dplyr::rename() explicitly - matrixStats::count() masks dplyr::count() — always use dplyr::count() explicitly - purrr::map() / mclust::map() collision — use lapply() + purrr::list_rbind() - methylKit crashes zero-variance sites — commaKit wraps , assigns p=1 - org.EcK12.eg.db requires :: syntax examples - Non-ASCII characters (e.g., x) cause R CMD check notes Core design decisions: - diffMethyl() loops mod_context, mod_type — prevents spurious pooling - Effect sizes always beta scale (0-1), M-value scale - Multiple testing correction genome-wide across mod_contexts - annotateSites() uses list-columns (CharacterList/IntegerList/NumericList) — revert single-match - commaData extends RangedSummarizedExperiment — genomic positions rowRanges() (GRanges) - mod_context derived demand mod_type + motif, stored column - Alignment genomic position (findOverlaps), string keys row names

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: commaKit (Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. accepts output modkit, Dorado, Megalodon, supports 6mA, 5mC, 4mC single unified container, covers full analysis workflow raw files quality control, site annotation, differential methylation testing, publication-quality visualization.

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: commaData S4 class — extends RangedSummarizedExperiment; stores beta values, coverage, sample metadata, genome info, annotations together. Three input types — modkit pileup BED (primary), Dorado BAM (full MM/ML tag decoding), Megalodon (legacy). Genome annotation via GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(); three modes: overlap, proximity, metagene (normalized position within feature bodies). Differential methylation — three statistical backends (methylKit, limma, quasi-binomial F-test), DESeq2-style interface (diffMethyl() → results() → filterResults()). Enrichment analysis — GO KEGG -representation analysis (ORA) gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) via clusterProfiler, offline KEGG support gene role semantics (target/regulator). Eight plot_*() functions — coverage QC, beta distributions, PCA, genome tracks, metagene profiles, TSS profiles, volcano plots, heatmaps; return ggplot objects customization. PCA uses variance-stabilized M-values internally supports return_data = TRUE custom plotting. mValues() — converts beta values read depths M-values (log2((M + ) / (U + ))), useful distance-based analyses custom plots. Compatible monoploid genome — genome annotations can loaded GFF3 BED files. Supports linear circular chromosomes. Multi-modification-type — 6mA, 5mC, 4mC coexist one object; every function accepts mod_type argument work individual types.

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: commaData extends RangedSummarizedExperiment constructed per-sample methylation files: built-synthetic dataset (588 sites, 6 samples, 100 kb genome) included testing demonstrations:
code: library(commaKit)  # Named vector: sample_name -> path to modkit pileup BED files <- c(   ctrl_1  = "path/to/ctrl_rep1.bed",   ctrl_2  = "path/to/ctrl_rep2.bed",   ctrl_3  = "path/to/ctrl_rep3.bed",   treat_1 = "path/to/treat_rep1.bed",   treat_2 = "path/to/treat_rep2.bed",   treat_3 = "path/to/treat_rep3.bed" )  col_data <- data.frame(   sample_name = c("ctrl_1",    "ctrl_2",    "ctrl_3",    "treat_1",    "treat_2",   "treat_3"),   condition   = c("control",   "control",   "control",   "treatment",  "treatment", "treatment"),   replicate   = c(1L,          2L,          3L,          1L,           2L,          3L) )  obj <- commaData(   files        = files,   colData      = col_data,   genome       = "path/to/genome.fa",   # FASTA, BSgenome, or named integer vector   annotation   = "path/to/genes.gff3",  # GFF3 or BED (optional)   motif        = "GATC",                # regex motif (optional)   min_coverage = 5L,   caller       = "modkit"               # "modkit", "dorado", or "megalodon" ) library(commaKit) data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 588 | samples: 6 #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA #> motifs: CCWGG, GATC #> mod contexts: 5mC_CCWGG, 6mA_GATC #> conditions: control, treatment #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total) #> annotation: 5 features #> motif sites: none #> caller: modkit #> min_coverage: 5

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: methylomeSummary() returns per-sample distribution statistics: plot_coverage() shows sequencing depth distribution per sample:  plot_methylation_distribution() plots density beta values, faceted modification type. Bacterial methylomes often show bimodal distribution (sites either fully methylated unmethylated):  plot_pca() runs PCA per-sample methylation profiles sample-level QC. Beta values converted M-values PCA better variance stabilization. Use return_data = TRUE retrieve scores data frame custom plotting:
code: ms <- methylomeSummary(comma_example_data) ms[, c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta",        "frac_methylated", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta frac_methylated n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.8654839   0.8929141       0.9897959       588 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.8705692   0.8959600       0.9948980       588 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.8638033   0.8918851       0.9897959       588 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.8357998   0.8864176       0.9421769       588 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8369054   0.8893089       0.9404762       588 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8388398   0.8866568       0.9455782       588 plot_coverage(comma_example_data) plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data) plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition") pca_df <- plot_pca(comma_example_data, return_data = TRUE) attr(pca_df, "percentVar")  # variance explained by PC1, PC2

code: annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, keep = "overlap")  # Fraction of sites overlapping at least one annotated feature si <- siteInfo(annotated) mean(lengths(si$feature_names) > 0) #> [1] 0.02891156 plot_metagene(comma_example_data, feature = "gene")

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. v1, formula must name one two-level comparison variable (e.g. ~ condition); multi-factor formulas ~ condition + batch interpreted yet. result object per-site statistics active result layer mirrored rowData: Extract results tidy data frame filter significant sites: plot_volcano() displays differential methylation landscape — effect size (delta methylation) versus significance (-log10 padj):  plot_heatmap() shows per-sample beta values top differentially methylated sites:
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") resultLayers(cd_dm) res <- results(cd_dm) sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Total 6mA sites tested:", nrow(res), "\n") #> Total 6mA sites tested: 588 cat("Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |delta_beta| >= 0.2):", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |delta_beta| >= 0.2): 31  # Top hits head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>       chrom position dm_delta_beta      dm_padj #> 196 chr_sim    50176    -0.7336497 1.849154e-75 #> 287 chr_sim    70003    -0.7050844 3.896483e-68 #> 260 chr_sim    63550    -0.7799241 5.006897e-66 #> 249 chr_sim    61440    -0.7090099 1.178364e-64 #> 347 chr_sim    86016    -0.6743832 3.661541e-62 #> 9   chr_sim     2180    -0.7543758 4.024452e-60 plot_volcano(res) plot_heatmap(res, cd_dm, n_sites = 30L)

title: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit
text: enrichMethylation() performs gene set enrichment differentially methylated genes. supports GO KEGG ontologies, ORA GSEA methods, distinguishes target genes (DM sites overlap) regulator genes (whose products bind near DM sites): KEGG enrichment, use offline path avoid API rate limits:
code: # Annotate sites first (required for enrichment) cd_dm <- annotateSites(cd_dm, keep = "overlap")  # GO enrichment on target genes enr <- enrichMethylation(cd_dm, ont = "BP", gene_role = "target") # Build KEGG term2gene mapping (2 API calls, cache to RDS) kegg_t2g <- buildKEGGTermGene("eco", file = "kegg_eco.rds")  # Build gene ID map (symbol <-> KEGG ID) id_map <- buildKEGGGeneIDMap("eco", OrgDb = org.EcK12.eg.db::org.EcK12.eg.db)  # Run KEGG enrichment with offline mapping enr_kegg <- enrichMethylation(cd_dm, kegg_term2gene = kegg_t2g$term2gene,                               kegg_term2name = kegg_t2g$term2name)

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
code: annotateSites(   object,   features = NULL,   feature_col = "feature_type",   name_col = "name",   window = 50L,   keep = c("all", "overlap", "proximity", "metagene"),   metadata_cols = NULL )

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: object commaData object. features GRanges genomic features annotate . NULL (default), annotation stored object via annotation(object) used. Must mcols columns named feature_col name_col. feature_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature type. Default: "feature_type". name_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature name. Default: "name". window Integer. Search window base pairs. features within distance site returned. Default: 50L. keep Character string controlling output columns retained: "" (default) Return four columns associations — including sites within window inside feature. "overlap" Subset site's associations features rel_position == 0 (inside feature). Drop rel_position frac_position columns. "proximity" Keep associations. Drop frac_position column. "metagene" Subset rel_position == 0. Drop rel_position column. Retain frac_position. metadata_cols Character vector NULL. Names additional mcols columns features pass parallel list columns rowData. column X stored X_values (CharacterList). Default: NULL.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: commaData object identical object except rowData extended annotation list columns. keep = "" (default): feature_types CharacterList. Feature type association per site. feature_names CharacterList. Feature name association per site. rel_position IntegerList. Signed relative position (bp): 0 inside, negative upstream, positive downstream. frac_position NumericList. Fractional position \([0, 1]\) inside features; NA outside. Intergenic sites (features within window) receive length-0 list elements columns.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: function searches within window bp site returns every feature found. Four parallel list columns added rowData: feature types, feature names, signed relative position, fractional position within feature. Signed rel_position convention (strand-aware): 0 — site inside feature. negative — site upstream (feature direction transcription). positive — site downstream (feature). frac_position \([0, 1]\) site inside feature (TSS = 0, TTS = 1, strand-aware) NA site outside. list columns strictly parallel: element \(j\) feature_types[[]] corresponds element \(j\) feature_names[[]], rel_position[[]], frac_position[[]]. invariant preserved metadata columns added via metadata_cols.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
code: data(comma_example_data) # Default: unified output with all four columns annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data) si <- siteInfo(annotated) # Sites inside at least one feature (rel_position == 0): sum(sapply(as.list(si$rel_position), function(x) any(x == 0L))) #> [1] 17 # Fractional position of first inside site: inside_idx <- which(lengths(si$frac_position) > 0)[1] si$frac_position[[inside_idx]] #> [1] 0.8857715  # Backward-compatible overlap output (no rel_position / frac_position): ann_ov <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, keep = "overlap") siteInfo(ann_ov)$feature_names[[1]] #> [1] "geneA"

text: files Named character vector mapping sample names file paths. Names must match colData$sample_name. Example: c(ctrl_1 = "/path//ctrl_1.bed", treat_1 = "/path//treat_1.bed"). colData data.frame one row per sample. Must contain columns sample_name replicate. condition column optional used design-aware functions diffMethyl requested formula. Additional columns (e.g., file_path, batch) preserved. genome Genome size information: named integer vector chromosome sizes (e.g., c(NC_000913 = 4641652L)), path FASTA file, DNAStringSet (Biostrings), BSgenome object. single-chromosome genomes pass BSgenome object directly named integer vector — index BSgenome $ (e.g., BSgenome.Ecoli.NCBI.20080805$NC_000913) yields DNAString chromosome name used. Set NULL omit genome information (recommended). multi-sequence source provided, genome info automatically restricted chromosomes present data. Chromosomes assumed circular default genome information attached, matching package's bacterial genome default; edit seqinfo(rowRanges(object)) construction chromosome treated linear. annotation Optional. Path GFF3 BED annotation file, pre-loaded GRanges object. NULL, annotation slot left empty. mod_type Optional character vector specifying modification types retain (e.g., "6mA" c("6mA", "5mC")). NULL, modification types detected files kept. motif Optional character string. DNA sequence motif (e.g., "GATC") locate genome using findMotifSites. results stored motifSites slot genome-wide GRanges motif instances. Requires genome FASTA path, DNAStringSet, BSgenome object (named integer vector). NULL, motifSites slot left empty. Note: argument distinct rowData(object)$motif, stores per-site sequence context extracted automatically modkit mod_code field (e.g., ",GATC,1" → motif = "GATC") NA Dorado Megalodon callers. expected_mod_contexts Named list NULL. provided, specifies modification type / sequence motif combinations retain. Names must modification type strings (e.g., "6mA", "5mC"). Values character vectors motif strings (e.g., c("GATC", "ACCACC")). Sites whose mod_context (paste(mod_type, motif, sep = "_")) match name–value pair dropped object assembled. message emitted reporting number sites dropped per modification type. Use NULL (default) retain sites. Example: list("6mA" = "GATC", "5mC" = c("CCWGG", "CCGG")). Note: Dorado/Megalodon callers motif NA, mod_context falls back just mod_type (e.g., "6mA"), sites retained include NA motif vector type (e.g., list("6mA" = NA)). min_coverage Integer. Minimum read depth include site. Sites present sample coverage threshold beta value set NA. Sites absent sample entirely also NA. Default 5. caller Character string specifying methylation caller produced input files. One "modkit" (default), "megalodon", "dorado".

what: commaKit: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit — commaKit-package
title: commaKit: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit — commaKit-package
text: commaKit package provides complete toolkit genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports three major modification types (6mA, 5mC, 4mC), handles input modkit pileup (primary), Dorado BAM, Megalodon (legacy) callers, provides unified data container, annotation utilities, differential methylation testing, full set visualization functions.

title: commaKit: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit — commaKit-package
text: Load data — commaData() constructs central commaData S4 object per-sample methylation files. QC — methylomeSummary(), coverageDepth(), plot_coverage(), plot_methylation_distribution(), plot_pca() provide sample-level quality assessment. Annotate — loadAnnotation() imports GFF3 BED file; annotateSites() maps methylation sites genomic features. Visualize — plot_genome_track() plot_metagene() show methylation genomic context. Differential methylation — diffMethyl() tests site; results() filterResults() extract filter results table; plot_volcano() plot_heatmap() visualize findings.

title: commaKit: Comparative Microbial Methylomics Analysis Kit — commaKit-package
text: commaData central S4 data container, extending SummarizedExperiment. Stores methylation (beta), coverage, modified-count, canonical-count matrices, per-site per-sample metadata, genome information, genomic annotation, motif site locations. assayLayers Tabular registry assay layers, provenance, default roles. resultLayers Tabular registry named differential methylation result runs.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: Default method (method = "methylkit"): Wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth(), uses logistic regression SLIM p-value correction. Robust small n, produces calibrated relative p-values suitable downstream filtering. Requires methylKit (BiocManager::install("methylKit")). Alternative model (method = "quasi_f"): per-site quasibinomial GLM empirical Bayes shrinkage dispersion estimates, analogous quasi-likelihood F-test edgeR. Fits quasibinomial GLM per site, collects per-site dispersion \(\hat\phi_j\) residual df \(df_j\), calls squeezeVar compute posterior dispersion estimates \(\{\tilde\phi_j\}\). T-statistics evaluated t-distribution \(d_0 + df_j\) degrees freedom. Requires limma. Alternative model (method = "limma"): Beta values transformed M-values via \(M = \log_2((n_{\mathrm{mod}} + \alpha) / (n_{\mathrm{unmod}} + \alpha))\), lmFit fits OLS model per site eBayes applies empirical Bayes variance shrinkage, borrowing information across sites stabilize per-site variance estimate. Recommended replicates (n < 3 per group). Requires limma (BiocManager::install("limma")). Effect sizes reported original beta scale. Method selection guidance: Use default "methylkit" backend want compatibility established methylKit workflows need results closely follow methylKit's logistic-regression conventions. Use "quasi_f" good general-purpose alternative bacterial methylomes want count-aware model empirical Bayes dispersion shrinkage genome-wide multiple-testing correction handled entirely inside commaKit. often good first alternative methylKit convergence warnings, zero-variance sites, runtime become distracting. Use "limma" want familiar limma empirical-Bayes linear-model workflow M-values, especially complete datasets replicates per group. three backends report dm_delta_beta original beta scale, effect sizes remain comparable even p-values differ. Multiple mod contexts: mod_context = NULL (default), modification contexts (mod_type x motif combinations) present object tested independently results combined. Sites belonging tested context receive NA dm_* columns. Testing mod_context rather just mod_type prevents spurious pooling biologically distinct methylation events (e.g., 6mA GATC Dam methyltransferase versus cytosine modifications GATC, likely artefactual). Small-sample note: Differential methylation testing replicates (e.g., n = 1 per group) mathematically possible extremely low statistical power. Treat results exploratory . Result columns added rowData: dm_pvalue Raw p-value GLM Wald test. dm_padj Adjusted p-value (Benjamini-Hochberg default). dm_delta_beta Effect size: mean methylation treatment group minus mean methylation reference (control) group. Positive values indicate hypermethylation treatment. dm_mean_beta_<condition> One column per condition level (named actual condition values), containing per-group mean beta value site. Analysis parameters result column names stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_params metadata(object)$diffMethyl_result_cols. Named result layers stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_results listed resultLayers(). default, active result layer also mirrored legacy dm_* columns rowData existing results() plotting workflows keep working.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
code: data(comma_example_data) # Test for differential 6mA methylation between conditions dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition, mod_type = "6mA") #> diffMethyl: testing 'condition' -- 'treatment' vs 'control' (reference) #> methylKit: comparing 'treatment' (treatment) vs 'control' (reference/control) #> uniting...  # How many sites are significant? rd <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::rowData(dm)) sum(rd$dm_padj < 0.05 & abs(rd$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 31

title: Parse a Dorado BAM file with MM/ML base modification tags — .parseDorado
text: function reads BAM using scanBam processes MM/ML tags aligned read. MM tag encodes bases carry modifications positions within read sequence (inter-base offsets); parallel ML tag provides modification probabilities (0–255 scaled 0–1). base called modified ML probability \(> 0.5\). Per-site statistics aggregated counting modified reads (\(n_{\text{mod}}\)) total reads genomic position (\(n_{\text{total}}\)). beta value \(\beta = n_{\text{mod}} / n_{\text{total}}\). CIGAR operations used map read positions reference coordinates. Soft-clipped (S) hard-clipped (H) bases excluded; insertions () consume read positions without advancing reference. Recommended workflow: users, simpler first run modkit pileup Dorado BAM, load resulting BED file caller = "modkit". Direct BAM parsing provided users prefer avoid modkit step.

title: Per-site quasi-likelihood F-test for differential methylation — .runQuasiF
text: limma must installed (listed Suggests). method runs three passes: Pass 1 — per-site GLM. quasibinomial model .betaBinomialTest fitted site: $$\mathrm{glm}(\mathrm{cbind}(n_{\mathrm{mod}},\, n_{\mathrm{unmod}})   \sim \mathrm{condition},\; \mathrm{family} = \mathrm{quasibinomial}())$$ site \(j\), three quantities collected: \(\hat\phi_j = \)fit\$dispersion — Pearson chi-squared dispersion estimate \(df_j = \)fit\$df.residual — residual degrees freedom \(\tilde{t}_j^{(0)} = t_j \times \sqrt{\hat\phi_j}\) — "unscaled" t-statistic (independent \(\hat\phi_j\)), \(t_j\) Wald t-statistic coef(summary(fit)) Pass 2 — empirical Bayes dispersion shrinkage. squeezeVar pools \(\{\hat\phi_j\}\) estimates across testable sites, fits log-normal prior, returns posterior dispersion estimates \(\{\tilde\phi_j\}\) prior degrees--freedom scalar \(d_0\). Pass 3 — moderated test statistic. posterior t-statistic p-value site : $$\tilde{t}_j = \frac{\tilde{t}_j^{(0)}}{\sqrt{\tilde\phi_j}},   \quad p_j = 2\,P(T \leq -|\tilde{t}_j|),\;   T \sim t(d_0 + df_j)$$ additional \(d_0\) degrees freedom power gain unadjusted quasibinomial test. procedure methodologically equivalent quasi-likelihood F-test edgeR (glmQLFTest), adapted methylation proportions (quasibinomial) rather RNA-seq counts (quasi-negative-binomial).

title: Gene set enrichment analysis of differential methylation results — enrichMethylation
text: method includes "gsea", per-gene score computed site-level statistics genes ranked decreasing order. "padj" \(-\log_{10}(\text{padj})\); significance , direction-agnostic. text: Computes per-position sliding window statistic (median mean) methylation beta values sample commaData object. genome size chromosome read genomeSizes(object), organism-specific values ever hardcoded. title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
text: object commaData object. Must genome size information genomeSizes(object) (.e., must constructed genome argument). window Positive integer. Window size base pairs. smoothed value position \(p\) computed positions \([p - \lfloor w/2 \rfloor,\; p + \lfloor w/2 \rfloor]\). stat Character string. Summary statistic apply within window. One "median" (default) "mean". mod_type Character vector NULL. provided, sites specified modification type(s) (e.g., "6mA", c("6mA", "5mC")) included smoothing. NULL (default), sites used. motif Character vector NULL. provided, sites matching sequence context motif(s) included (e.g., "GATC"). NULL (default), motifs included. mod_context Character vector NULL. provided, sites matching modification context included (e.g., "6mA_GATC"). Applied mod_type motif filters. circular Logical scalar NULL. NULL (default), circularity read seqinfo(object) chromosome. Chromosomes missing circularity metadata treated circular, matching package assumption bacterial genomes. TRUE FALSE, value overrides seqinfo(object) chromosomes. Circular chromosomes wrap windows across chromosome ends window position 1 can draw positions near chromosome end, vice versa.

title: commaKit 0.2.0
text: canonical package version 0.2.0. technical package identity changed comma commaKit; repository, documentation, installation URLs now use carl-stone/commaKit. exported commaData class comma_example_data dataset keep names API continuity.

title: commaKit 0.2.0
text: mod_context derived demand — mod_context computed mod_type motif (e.g. "6mA_GATC", "5mC_CCWGG") modContexts() siteInfo() rather stored persistent rowData column. motif NA (Dorado/Megalodon callers), fallback mod_type string alone (never "6mA_NA"). modContexts() — new exported S4 accessor returns sorted unique modification context strings commaData object. expected_mod_contexts constructor parameter — commaData() now accepts expected_mod_contexts, named list mapping mod types allowed motifs (e.g. list("6mA" = c("GATC", "ACCACC"), "5mC" = "CCWGG")). Sites unexpected mod_type × motif combinations dropped construction time informative message per mod type. filterSites(object, mod_context = ...) filter — filterSites() gains mod_context parameter filtering modification context. subset.commaData() remains deprecated compatibility forwards filterSites(). diffMethyl() loops mod_context — differential methylation now computed independently mod_context group (e.g. 6mA@GATC 6mA@ACCACC tested separately). mod_context parameter added diffMethyl(), results(), filterResults() context-specific extraction. mod_context parameter added throughout — methylomeSummary(), slidingWindow(), mValues(), writeBED(), eight plot_*() functions gain mod_context = NULL parameter context-level filtering. plot_tss_profile() additionally supports color_by = "mod_context" facet_by = "mod_context".

title: commaKit 0.2.0
text: Old commaData objects recreated — Schema v2 data model moved genomic positions rowRanges(), stores mod_type/motif row-range metadata, derives mod_context demand, stores caller minimum-coverage metadata metadata(object). Objects created earlier informal versions recreated source files using updated constructor.

title: commaKit 0.2.0
text: diffMethyl(method = "methylkit") longer crashes “object type ‘closure’ subsettable” modification context contains sites samples zero coverage filtering. methylKit::unite() retains sites united object; calculateDiffMeth() calls glm.fit -zero weights -NaN response, crashes. wrapper now filters sites calling calculateDiffMeth assigns p = 1 (consistent null hypothesis). Regression test added.

title: commaKit 0.2.0
text: Added siteCoverage() preferred package-specific coverage matrix accessor. coverage(commaData) remains deprecated compatibility coverage() already established Bioconductor meaning. Added filterSites() preferred high-level site/sample filter. subset.commaData() remains deprecated compatibility exporting broad subset() workflow conflicts base R expectations. Added explicit Author Maintainer fields DESCRIPTION R 4.6.0 compatibility. Replaced non-ASCII character writeBED() documentation ASCII equivalent. Updated .Rbuildignore exclude development files (.claude/, .codex/, .letta/, .lteams/, AGENTS.md, PRD.md, VISION.md, ROADMAP.md, SPECS.md). Renamed test files match test-functionName.R convention: test-coverageAnalysis.R → test-coverageDepth.R + test-varianceByDepth.R, test-enrichment.R → test-enrichMethylation.R, test-find_motif_sites.R → test-findMotifSites.R, test-load_annotation.R → test-loadAnnotation.R, test-m_values.R → test-mValues.R, test-plot_distribution.R → test-plot_methylation_distribution.R. Updated README: corrected example data size (588 sites), added enrichment analysis TSS profile workflow sections, updated feature list (eight plot functions, four DM backends, enrichment analysis), expanded roadmap table.
